---
title: "AgWise Forecast-Based Optimum Planting Date & Advisory Framework"
author: Leroux Louise, Mkuhlani Siyabusa, Ahmed Jemal Seid, Gosh Payel, Srivastava
  Amit
date: "2025-11-05"
output: html_document
theme : lumen
---

# <span style="color:#CD3333; background-color:#FFE4C4; font-family:helvetica;"> Introduction

<span style="font-family:helvetica; text-align:justify;">The AgWise platform offers a comprehensive, modular framework for generating **climate-informed, spatially targeted agricultural advisories** tailored to the needs of smallholder farmers across sub-Saharan Africa. It integrates remote sensing, seasonal climate forecasts, process-based crop simulation, and statistical analytics to produce decision-relevant outputs such as optimum planting dates, cultivar suitability maps, and yield forecasts. 

At the heart of AgWise is the **coupling of ECMWF SEAS5 seasonal forecasts and satellite-derived actual planting dates with the DSSAT crop model**. Bias-corrected and downscaled forecasts drive simulations initialized by satellite-derived Start of Season (SOS) indicators. The AgWISE planting date and cultivar framework allows to identify (1) the optimum planting window and best cultivar per location based on the current weather conditions, (2) the optimum planting window and best cultivar per location based on the weather forecast. This enables the system to capture interannual climate variability, anticipate risks like delayed onset or dry spells, and generate scenario-based advisories under multiple future conditions. 

AgWise supports a probabilistic, risk-aware advisory system that is robust under uncertainty by simulating multiple cultivars and planting dates across different ensemble members. The model outputs are validated through a multi-tiered approach using statistical metrics, remote sensing-based SOS benchmarks, and historical records from national institutions. Outputs are delivered through web dashboards, APIs, and mobile platforms to ensure accessibility and last-mile relevance.  </span>

# <span style="color:#CD3333; background-color:#FFE4C4; font-family:helvetica;"> The module architecture

<span style="font-family:helvetica; text-align:justify;"> The AgWISE planting date and cultivar module is organised in two main folders hosted in **agwise-planting-date-and-cultivar** (Figure 2):

* <b>Scripts</b> : It contains all the scripts needed to run the remote sensing, forecasting and crop model components of the module : <span style="color:blue;">*agwise-planting-date-and-cultivar/Scripts*
* <b>Data</b> : It contains the input and output data of the remote sensing, forecasting and crop model components of the module : <span style="color:blue;">*agwise-planting-date-and-cultivar/Data*
</span>

## <span style="color:#CD3333; font-family:helvetica;"> Scripts folder

<span style="font-family:helvetica; text-align:justify;"> The **Scripts** folder contains two sub-folders :

* <b>generic folder</b> : It contains generalized scripts while the instances of running the generic scripts for specific country, use case and crop combinations is host in the later. Each component has it's own subfolder :
    * <b>RS</b> : It contains the generic scripts of the remote sensing component - <span style="color:blue;"> *agwise-planting-date-and-cultivar/Scripts/generic/RS*
    * <b>Forecast</b> : It contains the generic scripts of the forecast component - <span style="color:blue;"> *agwise-planting-date-and-cultivar/Scripts/generic/Forecast*
    * <b>DSSAT</b> : It contains the generic scripts of the crop model component - <span style="color:blue;"> *agwise-planting-date-and-cultivar/Scripts/generic/DSSAT*
    
* <b> useCases folder </b> :In the module, for every country, use case and crop combinations, a unique folder is created to host all the scripts run for it.The generic structure is *useCaseName/Crop/Component* with component being RS for the remote sensing component, Forecast for the forecast component and DSSAT for the crop model component.

</span>

## <span style="color:#CD3333; font-family:helvetica;"> Data folder

<span style="font-family:helvetica; text-align:justify;"> The **Data ** folder contains a sub-folder for every country, use case, and crop  combinations, where all the data used as input or generated by the module (output) are stored. The generic structure is *useCaseName/Crop/*. Each use case folder contains 3 sub-folders :

* <b>raw </b> : It contains the unprocessed data used by the module. Please note that since the unprocessed NDVI data can be used for every crop of a specific use case, the data are stored at the useCase level. Each component of the module, RS, Forecast and DSSAT has its own sub-folder.
* <b>transform </b> : It contains intermediate results of processing data (eg. NDVI smoothed time series). Please note that since the processed NDVI data can be used for every crop of a specific use case, the data are stored at the useCase level. Each component of the module, RS, Forecast and DSSAT has its own sub-folder.
* <b>result </b> : It contains the final results of the module. Each component of the module, RS, Forecast and DSSAT has its own sub-folder.

![Figure 2. AgWISE planting date and cultivar module architecture](/home/jovyan/agwise-planting-date-and-cultivar/AgWISE_folder_architecture.png)

*To be updated when we have an idea how to organize it*

# <span style="color:#CD3333; background-color:#FFE4C4; font-family:helvetica;"> The Remote Sensing component

<span style="font-family:helvetica; text-align:justify;"> The objective of the **RS** (remote sensing) component is to provide information on **crop type probability areas and crop planting dates** based on EO and using cutting-edge data analytics. Crop type probability areas maps allow to have information on where each crops is predominantly grown and hence can be used to refine agronomic advises (eg. fertilizer recommendation) based on the areas actually covered by the crop. The crop planting dates information are used to optimize the simulation of the optimum planting dates by crop models by constraining the window of simulation using the historical statistical distribution of the actual planting dates as detected by remote sensing. The The RS component has been designed to be able to run at large scale (up to national scale) while using a minimum ground data as input. It relies on two type of information:  </span>  

* *Earth Observation*:  
    * <b>MODIS NDVI</b> (Normalized Difference Vegetation Index) : MODIS sensors provide the longest time series of information (ie. from 2000 to 2023) on vegetation development at a spatial resolution of 250-m and a 8-days temporal resolution. From 2024-onwards the module relies on VIIRS data.
    * <b>ESA World Cover </b> : providing land cover information, including cropland information, at 10-m spatial resolution. 
* *Ground data* :  
    * <b>On planting date</b> : providing information on the actual planting date as observed by farmers. Those data are used to calibrate and validate the actual planting date estimation.
    * <b>On crop type field location </b> : providing GPS coordinates of crop fields. Those data are used to train and validate the crop type model.  

</span>

## <span style="color:#CD3333; font-family:helvetica;"> The RS component scripts

<span style="color:blue;"> **Note:Phenology extraction and crop type probability mapping can be done only after downloading and pre processing the satellite data following the data sourcing steps**

    Make sure the scripts are run in the following order:
  
1. To download the MODIS/VIIRS data access  **get_MODISdata.R** - <span style="color:blue;"> *~/agwise-planting-date-and-cultivar/Scripts/generic/RS/get_MODISdata.R*
2. (optional) To download the Crop mask through GEE refer to **get_ESACropland_fromGEE.html** in <span style="color:blue;"> *~/agwise-datasourcing/dataops/datasourcing/Scripts/generic/* 
3. To process the NDVI data for any data gap access **get_MODISts_PreProc.R** - <span style="color:blue;"> *~/agwise-planting-date-and-cultivar/Scripts/generic/RS/get_MODISts_PreProc.R*
4. (optional) for crop type probability access **get_RS_Croptype.R** - <span style="color:blue;"> *~/agwise-planting-date-and-cultivar/Scripts/generic/RS/get_RS_Croptype.R*. </span> NOTA this step is optional, in case there is no ground data or not enough ground data to calibrate the crop type probability model, the following step (phenology extraction) can be run on the crop mask only (from GEE). 
5. To get the phenology (actual planting dates and harvesting dates) access **get_RS_Phenology.R** - <span style="color:blue;"> *~/agwise-planting-date-and-cultivar/Scripts/generic/RS/get_RS_Phenology.R*
6. To extract the crop planting dates statistical distribution for the CM location access **get_RStoCM_Phenology** - <span style="color:blue;"> *~/agwise-planting-date-and-cultivar/Scripts/generic/RS/get_RStoCM_Phenology.R*

## <span style="color:#CD3333; font-family:helvetica;"> How to run the RS component scripts

### Preparation of the environment
To ease the implementation for the RS component, a folder containing templates tu run the 6 RS component scripts for a specific use case (Country x Crop x Use Case) is providing in <span style="color:blue;"> *~/agwise-planting-date-and-cultivar/Scripts/useCases* </span>. Copy-paste the **useCase_Country_Templates** and rename the folder and all the scripts with the corresponding information of your use case : Country = Your targeted country (eg. Rwanda), Templates = Your use case name (eg. RAB), and Crop = your targeted crop (eg. Maize).

### Preprocessing of the RS data
The first step of the workflow is to preprocess the MODIS/VIIRS data, which includes the downloading of the MODIS/VIIRS data (script 1 : get_MODISdata_useCase_Country_Template.R), the downloading of the ESA WorldCover data from GEE (as an option)(script 2 : get_ESACropland_fromGEE.html) and the smoothing of MODIS/VIIRS data (script 3 : get_MODISts_PreProc_useCase_Country_Template.R).

* **get_MODISdata_useCase_Country_Template.R** : change the country,level,admin_unit_name ,useCaseName, Start_year, End_year based on your case study information. For deeper understanding of the different arguments please refer to the documentation in *get_MODISdata.R*.
* **get_ESACropland_fromGEE.html** (optional) : connect to your Google Earth Engine space and change the required information for your case study under 1a - Use case  Boundary section. The cropland mask has then to be uploaded under <span style="color:blue;">*agwise-planting-date-and-cultivar/Data/useCase_Country_useCaseName/RS/raw/CropMask*.
* **get_MODISts_PreProc_useCase_Country_Template.R** : change the country,level,admin_unit_name ,useCaseName, Planting_year, Harvesting_year based on your case study information. For deeper understanding of the different arguments please refer to the documentation in *get_MODISts_PreProc.R*.

### Actual planting and harvesting dates
The second step of the workflow is to process the MODIS/VIIRS data in order to obtain the actual planting dates for each crop modelling location, which includes the crop type probability map (as an option)(script 4 : get_RS_CropType_useCase_Country_Template_Crop.R), the computation of the planting and harvesting dates (script 5 :get_RS_Phenology_useCase_Country_Template_Crop.R), and the extraction of the actual planting dates for each location of the CM component (script 6 : get_RStoCM_Phenology_useCase_Country_Template_Crop.R).

* **get_RS_CropType_useCase_Country_Template_Crop.R** (optional) : change the country,level,admin_unit_name ,useCaseName, Planting_year, Harvesting_year, Planting_month, Harvesting_month and crop based on your case study information. For deeper understanding of the different arguments please refer to the documentation in *get_RS_CropType.R*.

**IMPORTANT** : The ground data containing the GPS location of the planting dates data must follow specific format : (1)- Should be a .RDS file, named **data4RS.DRS**, (2) Should contain at least 5 columns : 'lon' (longitude data), 'lat' (latitude data), 'planting_date' (planting date data in the **Dates format** YYYY-mm-dd), 'harvest_date' (harvest date data in the **Dates format** YYYY-mm-dd), and 'crop' (the type of crop). This file has to be located under under <span style="color:blue;">*agwise-planting-date-and-cultivar/Data/useCase_Country_useCaseName/RS/raw/*.

* **get_RS_Phenology_useCase_Country_Template_Crop.R**  : change the country,level,admin_unit_name ,useCaseName, Planting_year, Harvesting_year, Planting_month, Harvesting_month, emergence, and thr based on your case study information. For deeper understanding of the different arguments please refer to the documentation in *get_RS_Phenology.R*.

**IMPORTANT** : The ground data containing the GPS location of the planting dates data must follow specific format : (1)- Should be a .RDS file, named **data4RS.DRS**, (2) Should contain at least 5 columns : 'lon' (longitude data), 'lat' (latitude data), 'planting_date' (planting date data in the **Dates format** YYYY-mm-dd), 'harvest_date' (harvest date data in the **Dates format** YYYY-mm-dd), and 'crop' (the type of crop). This file has to be located under under <span style="color:blue;">*agwise-planting-date-and-cultivar/Data/useCase_Country_useCaseName/RS/raw/*.

* **get_RStoCM_Phenology_useCase_Country_Template_Crop.R** : change the country,useCaseName, and crop based on your case study information. For deeper understanding of the different arguments please refer to the documentation in *get_RStoCM_Phenology.R*.

### A complete example : Country = Rwanda, useCase = RAB, crop = Maize

```
## Script 1 : get_MODISdata_useCase_Rwanda_RAB.R
#################################################################################################################
## source "get_MODISdata.R" function and execute it for Rwanda RAB use case
#################################################################################################################

source("~/agwise-planting-date-and-cultivar/Scripts/generic/RS/get_MODISdata.R")

Start_year <- seq (2021, 2022) #To be changed seq(first year of analysis, last year of analysis)
End_year <- seq (2022, 2023) #To be changed seq(first year of analysis, last year of analysis)

for (i in 1:length(Start_year) {
    download_MODIS(country = Rwanda, useCaseName = RAB,level=0, admin_unit_name = NULL, Start_year = Start_year[i], End_year=End_year[i], overwrite = TRUE)
}

## Script 3 : get_MODISts_PreProc_useCase_Rwanda_RAB.R
#################################################################################################################
## source "get_MODISts_PreProc.R" function and execute it for Rwanda RAB use case
#################################################################################################################

source("~/agwise-planting-date-and-cultivar/Scripts/generic/RS/get_MODISts_PreProc.R")

Planting_year=seq(2002,2022) #To be changed seq(first year of analysis, last year of analysis)
Harvesting_year=seq(2003,2023) #To be changed seq(first year of analysis, last year of analysis)

for (i in 1:length(Planting_year)){
  smooth_rasterTS(country = Rwanda, useCaseName = RAB,Planting_year = Planting_year[i], Harvesting_year = Harvesting_year[i], overwrite = TRUE, CropMask=TRUE)
}


## Script 4 : get_RS_CropType_useCase_Rwanda_RAB_Maize.R
#################################################################################################################
## source "get_RS_Croptype.R" functions and execute it for Rwanda RAB Maize use case
#################################################################################################################

source("~/agwise-potentialyield/dataops/potentialyield/Script/generic/RemoteSensing/get_RS_Croptype.R")

country = "Rwanda"
useCaseName = "RAB"
level = 1
admin_unit_name = NULL
Planting_year = 2021
Harvesting_year = 2022
Planting_month = "September"
Harvesting_month = "June"
overwrite = TRUE
crop = c("Maize")
coord = c("lon", "lat")
CropMask = FALSE

CropType (country, useCaseName, level, admin_unit_name, Planting_year, Harvesting_year, Planting_month, Harvesting_month, crop, coord, overwrite, CropMask)

## Script 5 : get_RS_Phenology_useCase_Rwanda_RAB_Maize.R
#################################################################################################################
## source "get_RS_Phenology.R" function and execute it for Rwanda RAB Maize use case
#################################################################################################################

source("~/agwise-potentialyield/dataops/potentialyield/Script/generic/RemoteSensing/get_RS_Phenology.R")

country = "Rwanda"
useCaseName = "RAB"
level = 1
admin_unit_name = NULL
crop= "Maize"
Planting_year = 2021
Harvesting_year = 2022
Planting_month = "September"
Harvesting_month = "March"
emergence = 5
overwrite = TRUE
CropMask = TRUE
CropType = TRUE
thr= c(0.50, 0.30)
validation = TRUE
coord = c('lon', 'lat')

Phenology_rasterTS(country, useCaseName, crop, level, admin_unit_name, Planting_year, Harvesting_year, Planting_month, Harvesting_month, 
                   emergence, CropMask=T, CropType=T, coord, thr=c(0.50,0.30), validation=T, overwrite)
                   
## Script 6 : get_RStoCM_Phenology_useCase_Rwanda_RAB_Maize.R
#################################################################################################################
## source "get_RStoCM_Phenology.R" function and execute it for Maize RAB Maize use case
#################################################################################################################

source("~/agwise-planting-date-and-cultivar/Scripts/generic/RS/get_RStoCM_Phenology.R")

country <- "Rwanda"
useCaseName <- "Rwanda"
crop <- "Maize"
coord <- c('lon', 'lat')
CropModelName <- "DSSAT"
overwrite=TRUE

RSplantingWindow(country, useCaseName, crop, coord, CropModelName, overwrite=TRUE)
```

# <span style="color:#CD3333; background-color:#FFE4C4; font-family:helvetica;"> The Forecast component

<span style="font-family:helvetica; text-align:justify;"> The objective of the **Forecast** component **TO BE FILLED BY JEMAL** 
</span>

## <span style="color:#CD3333; font-family:helvetica;"> The Forecast component scripts

    Make sure the scripts are run in the following order:

# <span style="color:#CD3333; background-color:#FFE4C4; font-family:helvetica;"> The Crop modelling component

<span style="font-family:helvetica; text-align:justify;"> **Background**: The objective of the **CM** (crop model) component to generate spatially explicit crop yield simulations using process-based models. These simulations are based on predefined cropping windows from the remote sensing component and cultivar specific parameters. The modelling is conducted at varying spatial scales, typically at  resolution. The spatial crop modelling framework, APSIM and DSSAT workflows have been set up for spatial modelling. This module predominantly uses the DSSAT crop model. APSIM model will coupled with RS and seasonal forecasting in the near future. The current coupling is based on DSAT. The crop modelling component relies on 3 types of input data:

**Cultivar information**: Cultivar coefficients are generated through calibration and validation process using long term experimental data sets that include historical crop yields, crop management and agro-ecological conditions. These coefficients represent the genetic parameters needed by DSSAT or APSIM to accurately simulate crop growth.

**Geospatial data**: Once calibrated, the crop model is spatialized by coupling it with geospatial weather and soil datasets. The key geospatial inputs include: Rainfall-Min and Max temperature (CHIRPS); Solar radiation (AgERA5); Soil data (Soil grids). 

**Seasonal forecast**:The seasonal forecast data is at a 9-month horizon (ECMWF). The crop yield simulations the data will be analysed to determine optimum sowing dates for specific cultivars and predicted seasonal forecast information.  
</span>

## <span style="color:#CD3333; font-family:helvetica;"> The CM component scripts

    Make sure the scripts are run in the following order:

1. To source the temperature, rainfall and solar radiation data from the geospatial files into intermediary files access **get_geoSpatialData_Rwanda_Maize.R** - <span style="color:blue;"> *~/agwise-planting-date-and-cultivar/Scripts/generic/DSSAT/get_geoSpatialData_Rwanda_Maize.R*. Please note at this stage we are still using historical weather data for Chirps-Rainfall, AgERA (Temperature, Radiation).This uses the coordinate structure from RS which specifies only areas and corresponding coordinates under crop land (step 6 of the RS component). 
<span style="color:red;"> Louise comment : This script is not generic --> needs to be generic
2. To convert weather and soil data into DSSAT model ready format access **01_get_CM_geo_RAB_Maize.R**. <span style="color:blue;"> *~/agwise-planting-date-and-cultivar/Scripts/generic/DSSAT/01_get_CM_geo_RAB_Maize.R*. It formats intermediary data into soil and weather DSSAT model ready formats, per each variety, Admin 1 level and per coordinate.
<span style="color:red;"> Louise comment : This script is not generic --> The generic one is readGeo_CM_zone.R, needs to rework that script to be generic
3. To generate experimental input files and run simulations access **02_run_DSSAT_Rwanda_RAB_Maize_RS.R**. <span style="color:blue;"> *~/agwise-planting-date-and-cultivar/Scripts/generic/DSSAT/02_run_DSSAT_Rwanda_RAB_Maize_RS.R*. </span>
This script works in two parts :
    * Part A of the script : Uses a DSSAT experimental template and modifies the sowing window based on the RS inputs. Specifically, modifies the weather and soil input information as well as the initial conditions and treatment structure. This modifies and creates a template file for each coordinate.  
    * Part B of the script : Runs the simulation per each of the coordinates looping through the different states and varieties. 
    
<span style="color:red;"> Louise comment : This script is not generic --> We needs to rework that script to be generic. </span>

4. To summarize the season's outputs of planting and variety based on ENSO phases access **03_summary_new_ONI_crop_phases.R **. <span style="color:blue;"> *~/agwise-planting-date-and-cultivar/Scripts/generic/DSSAT/03_summary_new_ONI_crop_phases.R*</span>. This aggregates all simulation outputs into a single tibble and also characterises the season’s outputs of planting date and variety based on ENSO phases. The tibble is further processed into optimum sowing and variety dates.

<span style="color:red;"> Louise comment : This script is not generic --> We needs to rework that script to be generic
